mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger corporate earnings revisions downward, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the opposite direction over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of housing starts and consumer credit outstanding indicates a synchronized withdrawal of household spending power. This dual demand shock forces equity valuations to repricing lower, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both decline for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly lower when both series contract simultaneously compared to periods where only one contracts, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of peak industrial production, a subsequent monthly decline in the industrial production index signals supply chain bottlenecks resolving into excess capacity. This supply glut compresses corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when INDPRO declines below its 12-month average during confirmed capacity expansion phases, or if the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-term trend rate of 2.5 percent demonstrates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth. This fundamental strength translates directly into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings momentum accelerates.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative or zero predictive power when GDP growth exceeds 2.5% but SPX 5-day forward returns are consistently negative, or the rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 for three consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 5.0 percent, the cost of capital exceeds the historical average return on equity, forcing equity risk premiums to expand. This valuation compression generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, or the signal's information ratio drops below 0.5 over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate and decline in total nonfarm payrolls confirms a structural labor market contraction rather than cyclical noise. This dual deterioration reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate hiring, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises and PAYEMS falls in the same month
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both series move in opposite directions, or the predictive accuracy of the interaction term falls below 45% in a 3-year rolling walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing market peaking regime, a month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5 percent acts as a leading indicator of broader consumer confidence collapse. This rapid demand reversal triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as market participants price in a consumption slowdown.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if SPX 5-day forward returns are positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero when HOUST drops by more than 5% MoM, or if the signal's directional consistency falls below 50% over any 24-month period.
